_doc: Cached starting-prices (v2: per-entry native currency) AND access metadata for POIs in assets/js/*-pois.js. Keyed by POI name (n field). Refreshed on deploy by tools/fetch_poi_prices.py + .github/workflows/refresh-poi-prices.yml. Manually-curated entries (source_kind=manual) are NEVER overwritten by the auto-refresher; only source_kind=wikidata price entries are updated. Optional access fields (url, car_status, parking, entrance, getting_there, operational) are read by app.js's loadPoiPrices to surface real-world parking + entrance info in the POI popup and to redirect 'Open in Google Maps' to the parking lat/lng rather than the POI's display coords for cable-car/car-free destinations.

21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art.notes: Hours: Exhibition Zone 10:00–18:00 (until 20:00 Fri/Sat), closed Mon. Free 'Public Zone' (incl. Swimming Pool exterior view). Paid Exhibition Zone varies by show, typically ¥450–1200 adult.

parking.name: Kanazawa 21st Century Museum Underground Parking
parking.notes: Direct access below museum; entrance off Hirosaka.

21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art.getting_there: From Kanazawa Stn: Kenrokuen Shuttle Bus or Hokuriku Tetsudō bus to Hirosaka/21st Century Museum (~10 min).

Abashiri Prison Museum.notes: Open-air museum of the former Abashiri Prison; hours 09:00–17:00 (last entry 16:00).
Abashiri Prison Museum.getting_there: Drive ~15 min from central Abashiri / Abashiri Station; large free lot at the museum entrance. Buses from JR Abashiri Station also stop at Hakubutsukan Abashiri Kangoku.

Abbey of Saint Gall.notes: Library + cathedral combined ticket; cathedral alone is free.

Achi Village Starry Sky (Heavens Sonohar.notes: Heavens Sonohara gondola + Star Village Achi night tour from ¥3,300 adult; price varies by season. Reservation recommended.
Achi Village Starry Sky (Heavens Sonohar.getting_there: Drive via Chuo Expressway Iida-Yamamoto IC, ~15 min to Heavens Sonohara base. Shuttle buses run from Iida Station in season.

Adachi Museum of Art.notes: Adult ¥2,500 confirmed on official EN site (Apr 2026). Hours: 09:00–17:30 (Apr–Sep), 09:00–17:00 (Oct–Mar). Open daily.

parking.name: Adachi Museum of Art free parking
parking.notes: Free on-site lot for 400 cars + 30 buses.

Adachi Museum of Art.getting_there: Free shuttle bus from JR Yasugi Stn (~20 min, ~2/h). Yasugi is 15 min by ltd-express from Matsue. By car: 10 min from Yasugi IC (San-in Expwy).

Aiguille du Midi.notes: Cable-car return ticket €83 adult (summer 2026) ≈ CHF 79. Pre-book a boarding number online.

parking.name: Parking Centre Ville – Grépon (Chamonix)
parking.notes: Largest outdoor lot in Chamonix (800 spaces summer / 500 winter). Height limit 2.2 m. EV charging on-site. Address: 200 Chemin à Batioret.

entrance.name: Aiguille du Midi cable-car valley station (Place de l'Aiguille du Midi)
entrance.transfer: ~210 m, 3-min walk north-northeast from Grépon parking

Aiguille du Midi.getting_there: Park at Grépon outdoor lot in Chamonix (800 spaces); 3-min walk to the cable-car valley station (1,035 m); two-stage cable car rises 2,807 m to 3,842 m in ~20 min. Pre-book online.

parking.name: Parking Arc 2000 (outdoor resort surface lot)
parking.notes: Expensive resort surface parking at Arc 2000 (~2000 m). Rate estimated. Alternative: free P+R at Bourg-Saint-Maurice rail station → funicular to Arc 1600 (€6 each way, 7 min) → free intra-resort shuttle bus to Arc 2000.

entrance.name: Varet gondola base — Arc 2000 (summer access to Aiguille Rouge)
entrance.transfer: 200 m walk from car park; Varet gondola to ~2,770 m, then Aiguille Rouge cable car to 3,227 m summit

Aiguille Rouge.getting_there: From Bourg-Saint-Maurice: D87E10 (~14 km) to Arc 2000 (expensive parking). Varet gondola + Aiguille Rouge cable car to 3,227 m. Alt: BSM funicular → Arc 1600 → free inter-resort bus to Arc 2000.

Ainokura Gasshō Village.notes: Hours: Village 24h; folklore museum 08:30–17:00. Free to walk village (donation requested). Parking ¥500. UNESCO World Heritage gasshō hamlet.

parking.name: Ainokura Visitor Parking
parking.notes: Upper lot above village; 3-min walk down to hamlet (no cars in village).

Ainokura Gasshō Village.getting_there: Kaetsunō Bus from Takaoka Stn (Johana line + bus) to Ainokura-guchi (~75 min), 5-min walk uphill.

Akame 48 Waterfalls.notes: Trail entry incl. Japanese Salamander Center ¥500 adult; hours ~08:30–17:00 (seasonal).
Akame 48 Waterfalls.getting_there: Drive ~10 min from Meihan Expressway Hari IC, or bus ~10 min from Kintetsu Akameguchi Station to Akame-taki bus stop.

Akashi Kaikyō Bridge.notes: Outdoor viewing from Maiko Park / Awaji side free. Bridge Exhibition Center + Maiko Marine Promenade walkway ~¥250–300; full Bridge World Tour (tower-top) ~¥5,000 (reservation, seasonal).
Akashi Kaikyō Bridge.getting_there: JR Maiko Station (5 min walk to Maiko Marine Promenade) or drive via Daini Shinmei Road / Honshu-Shikoku Expressway.

Akita-Komagatake.notes: Free mountain access; in summer (early Jun–mid Oct) private cars are restricted on the Komagatake Line — shuttle bus from Alpa-Komakusa ~¥1,050 RT to 8th Station trailhead.
Akita-Komagatake.getting_there: Drive to Alpa-Komakusa near Tazawako, then mandatory shuttle bus up the Komagatake Line in restricted season.

Akiu Great Falls.notes: Free 24h outdoor viewing; one of Japan's top three waterfalls.
Akiu Great Falls.getting_there: Drive ~40 min from central Sendai via Route 286/48; seasonal bus from JR Sendai Station to Akiu Otaki stop.

Akiyoshidō Cave.getting_there: Drive ~20 min from Chugoku Expressway Mine-Higashi IC; buses from JR Shin-Yamaguchi Station to Akiyoshido bus terminal.

Albula Railway.notes: Chur–St. Moritz one-way; covered by Swiss Travel Pass.

Aletsch Glacier.notes: Cable-car ticket varies by gateway (Bettmeralp / Riederalp / Fiescheralp).

parking.name: Bettmeralp Talstation P+R (Betten valley station)
parking.notes: Parking at the cable-car base in Betten. Bettmeralp village is car-free. Alternatives: Mörel (→ Riederalp) or Fiesch (→ Fiescheralp/Eggishorn 2,869 m, highest viewpoint).

entrance.name: Bettmeralp Talstation – cable car to Bettmeralp
entrance.transfer: Cable car from Betten to car-free Bettmeralp (1,931 m), then Bettmerhorn gondola to 2,647 m glacier viewpoint

Aletsch Glacier.getting_there: Park at Bettmeralp Talstation in Betten; cable car to car-free Bettmeralp (1,931 m); Bettmerhorn gondola to 2,647 m for panoramic glacier views (Europe's longest, UNESCO).

parking.name: Mörel — Riederalp Bergbahn P+R
parking.notes: Parking at the Mörel gondola valley station for Riederalp West/Mitte (confirmed by Aletsch Arena: 'parking facilities in Mörel right next to the valley station'). Riederalp, Bettmeralp, and Fiescheralp are car-free.

entrance.name: Mörel — Riederalp gondola valley station (800 m)
entrance.transfer: Gondola to car-free Riederalp (8 min, 1,930 m); 20–30-min hike south into the UNESCO Aletschwald primeval forest along the glacier's northern moraine

Aletschwald (Aletsch Forest).getting_there: Park at Mörel Riederalp P+R (CHF 10/day est.); 8-min gondola to car-free Riederalp; 30-min hike into the UNESCO Aletschwald primeval forest flanking Europe's longest glacier.

Amakusa Dolphin Watching.notes: ~¥3,000 adult boat tour, ~1 hour; multiple operators at Itsuwa/Futae harbors near Tsuji Island. Reservation recommended.
Amakusa Dolphin Watching.getting_there: Drive across Amakusa Five Bridges to Itsuwa-machi on Kamishima; ~45 min from Hondo. Bus access limited.

Amami Ōshima Mangrove Forest.notes: Mangrove Park entry free; canoe tour ~¥2,000–3,000 adult. Hours ~09:00–17:30.
Amami Ōshima Mangrove Forest.getting_there: Drive ~1 hr south from Amami Airport / Naze on Route 58 to Sumiyo. Limited bus service.

Amanohashidate.notes: Hours: Sandbar 24h; Kasamatsu cable car/chairlift 09:00–17:00 (¥800 RT adult); Amanohashidate View Land monorail 09:00–17:00 (¥850 RT). Sandbar free to walk/cycle.

parking.name: Chionji Parking (Amanohashidate south)
parking.notes: Near Chion-ji temple at south end of sandbar, by Amanohashidate Stn.

Amanohashidate.getting_there: Kyoto Tango Railway to Amanohashidate Stn (~2 hr from Kyoto via JR Hashidate ltd express). Sandbar 5-min walk from station.

Ameya-Yokochō (Ameyoko) Market.getting_there: JR Ueno or Okachimachi Station; market runs along the JR tracks between them.

Aogashima Caldera.notes: Remote inhabited volcanic island ~360 km south of Tokyo; outdoor caldera views free. Access often weather-cancelled.
Aogashima Caldera.getting_there: Fly ANA to Hachijōjima, then Aogashima-mura ferry Kuroshio-maru (~3 hr, weather-dependent, ~50% cancellation) or Tokyo Reito helicopter (9 seats, reservation essential).

Aoike (Jūniko).notes: Free outdoor access to Aoike and the Jūniko lake walking trails within Shirakami-Sanchi.
Aoike (Jūniko).getting_there: Drive ~20 min from JR Jūniko Station via Route 101; seasonal Konan bus to Okuniko-iriguchi.

Aoshima & Devil's Washboard.notes: Free pedestrian causeway to Aoshima island and the Oni-no-Sentakuita (Devil's Washboard) rock formations.
Aoshima & Devil's Washboard.getting_there: JR Aoshima Station (5 min walk) or drive ~30 min south of central Miyazaki on Route 220.

Arashiyama Bamboo Grove.notes: Free public path through bamboo. Hours: open 24 h (no gate). Best visited early morning to avoid crowds.

parking.name: Nison-in / Arashiyama coin parking (Tenryū-ji area)
parking.notes: Multiple coin lots near Tenryū-ji and Togetsukyō bridge; ~5–10 min walk to grove.

Arashiyama Bamboo Grove.getting_there: 5 min walk from JR Saga-Arashiyama Stn (Sagano Line) or 10 min from Hankyu/Keifuku Arashiyama Stns. From Kyoto Stn ~15 min by JR.

Arima Onsen.notes: Town strolling free; Kin-no-Yu (gold-water) public bath ¥800 adult, Gin-no-Yu (silver) ¥600. Closed 2nd & 4th Tue.
Arima Onsen.getting_there: Kobe Electric Railway to Arima-Onsen Station, or drive via Hanshin Expressway Route 7 Kita-Kobe. Shinki bus from Sannomiya/Osaka.

Art House Project (Honmura).notes: Multi-site Art House Project ticket (6 houses excl. Kinza). Hours: 10:00–16:30, closed Mon (open if holiday, closed next day). Kinza & Minamidera need reservation. Tickets at Honmura Lounge.

parking.name: Naoshima Honmura Port parking
parking.notes: Walk 5 min to Art House sites

Art House Project (Honmura).getting_there: Ferry from Uno Port (Okayama) or Takamatsu Port to Naoshima Miyanoura/Honmura. Town bus or walk from Honmura ferry stop (~5 min).

Asahi-dake.notes: Asahidake Ropeway round trip ¥3,200 adult (peak Jun–Oct); ¥2,200 off-season. Mountain itself free to hike.
Asahi-dake.getting_there: Drive ~1 hr from Asahikawa via Route 213 to Asahidake Onsen; Ide-yu-go bus from JR Asahikawa Station ~1 hr 30 min.

Asahiyama Zoo.notes: Adult admission ¥1,000; hours 09:30–17:15 (summer), 10:30–15:30 (winter); night zoo in mid-Aug.

parking.name: City-run lots
parking.notes: Private adjacent lots ~¥500/day

Asahiyama Zoo.getting_there: Drive ~30 min from central Asahikawa or Asahikawa Airport; Asahikawa Denki Kido bus from JR Asahikawa Station ~40 min.

Asakusa Shrine (Sanja-sama).notes: Free shrine grounds adjacent to Sensō-ji. Hours: 09:00–16:30 (office/amulets); grounds open 24h. Sanja Matsuri held 3rd weekend of May.
Asakusa Shrine (Sanja-sama).getting_there: Tokyo Metro Ginza Line / Toei Asakusa Line / Tobu Skytree Line to Asakusa Stn; 5-min walk through Nakamise.

Ashikaga Flower Park — Great Wisteria.notes: Admission varies by bloom (¥400–¥2,200); peak wisteria season (mid-Apr–mid-May) up to ¥2,200 adult. Hours 09:00–18:00 (extended in season).
Ashikaga Flower Park — Great Wisteria.getting_there: JR Ashikaga Flower Park Station (Ryomo Line, adjacent); or drive via Kita-Kanto Expressway Ashikaga IC ~10 min.

Aso Caldera.notes: Caldera viewpoints free; Naka-dake crater visit requires Aso-san Park Road toll: car ¥1,000, motorcycle ¥400. Subject to closure on volcanic alerts. Hours summer 08:30–18:00.

parking.name: Aso-san Naka-dake Crater Parking
parking.notes: Closed when alert level ≥2

Aso Caldera.getting_there: From JR Aso Stn (Hōhi Line) take Sanko bus to Aso-san Nishi (~35 min) then walk or shuttle to crater. Self-drive via Aso-san Park Road.

Aso Shrine.notes: Free precinct visit; ongoing reconstruction since 2016 Kumamoto earthquake. Open dawn–dusk.
Aso Shrine.getting_there: JR Miyaji Station (~10 min walk) on Hohi Main Line; or drive via Kyushu Expressway Kumamoto IC + Route 57 (~1 hr).

Atsuta Jingū.notes: Free shrine precincts open 24h. Treasure Hall (Hōmotsukan) ¥500 adult, 09:00–16:30, closed last Wed-Thu monthly. Houses sacred sword Kusanagi.

parking.name: Atsuta Jingū East Parking
parking.notes: East/West/South lots

Atsuta Jingū.getting_there: Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line to Jingū-mae Stn (3 min walk) or JR Tōkaidō Line to Atsuta Stn (8 min walk). Subway Meijō Line: Jingū-Nishi.

Augusta Raurica.notes: Open-air Roman site is free; museum CHF 8.

Avenches — Roman Amphitheatre (Aventicum.notes: Amphitheatre is free; Roman Museum CHF 6.

Basel Minster & Altstadt.notes: Cathedral and old town free; Pfalz tower CHF 6.

parking.name: Parcheggio San Giovanni (Via Valassina / village entrance)
parking.notes: Very limited parking at southern village entrance. Fills by 09:00 in peak summer. A small additional lot exists at Loppia (north end). Strongly recommended alternative: car-ferry from Varenna (€4.60 pp + €4/car, ~10 min) or passenger ferry from Como (~1 h). navigazionelaghi.it.

entrance.name: Piazza Mazzini / Bellagio ferry pier
entrance.transfer: ~10-min walk from fringe parking; or Navigazione Laghi ferry from Varenna/Como/Lecco/Menaggio

Bellagio.getting_there: Drive to San Giovanni fringe lot (very limited, fills fast); better: car-ferry from Varenna (~10 min, €4.60 pp + €4/car) or passenger ferry from Como (~1 h). navigazionelaghi.it.

Benesse House Museum.notes: Adult ¥1,300 (15 & under free). Hours: 09:30–16:00 (last entry 15:30); Valley Gallery included. Free for hotel guests. Tadao Ando architecture.
Benesse House Museum.getting_there: Ferry to Naoshima Miyanoura Port; Benesse Art Site free shuttle bus to museum (~15 min). Cars cannot enter Benesse area — park at Miyanoura.

Beppu Jigoku Meguri (Seven Hells).notes: Combined 7-Hells pass ¥2,400 adult (since Feb 2025) — single hell ¥500. Hours: 08:00–17:00 daily. Buy at Umi-Jigoku or Chinoike-Jigoku.

parking.name: Umi Jigoku free parking
parking.notes: Free lots at each Kannawa-area hell; Chinoike & Tatsumaki are ~3 km north (separate lot).

Beppu Jigoku Meguri (Seven Hells).getting_there: Bus 5/9/41 from JR Beppu Stn West Exit to Umi-Jigoku-mae / Kannawa (~20 min). By car: 5 min from Beppu IC (Oita Expwy).

Bergamo Alta (Città Alta).notes: ATB funicular ticket €1.70 single (75 min) ≈ CHF 2; 24-hr pass €3.50.

parking.name: Città Bassa — parking near funicular lower station
parking.notes: ZTL bars non-residents from driving into Città Alta. Park in Bergamo Bassa (blue-zone street parking or covered lot near the funicular lower station at Viale Vittorio Emanuele II / Via Sudorno). ATB funicular (Linea fun.c) is included in standard ATB transit pass.

entrance.name: Città Alta — Piazza Mercato delle Scarpe (funicular upper station)
entrance.transfer: ATB funicular (~4 min); ticket €1.70 single (75 min)

Bergamo Alta (Città Alta).getting_there: Park in Bergamo Bassa near funicular lower station (ZTL bars non-residents from Città Alta). ATB funicular €1.70 single (75 min) or 24-hr pass €3.50.

Bernina Express.notes: Chur–Tirano one-way base fare; mandatory seat reservation extra (~CHF 26 in summer).

Bettmeralp.notes: Cable car return from Betten Talstation to Bettmeralp.

Bitchū-Matsuyama Castle, Takahashi.notes: Castle keep admission ¥500 adult; hours 09:00–17:30 (Apr–Sep), 09:00–16:30 (Oct–Mar). Highest original-tenshu mountaintop castle in Japan.
Bitchū-Matsuyama Castle, Takahashi.getting_there: JR Bitchū-Takahashi Station, then taxi or shuttle bus to Fuigo-toge, then ~20 min uphill walk to keep.

Blue Pond (Aoiike).notes: Free outdoor viewing; winter light-up nightly ~17:00–21:00 (Nov–Apr).
Blue Pond (Aoiike).getting_there: Drive ~20 min from Biei or ~40 min from Asahikawa via Route 966; seasonal Dohoku bus from JR Biei Station to Shirogane Aoiike-iriguchi.

Borromean Islands.notes: Adult entry: Isola Bella €22 ≈ CHF 21; Isola Madre €19; combined ticket €34.

parking.name: Stresa lakefront / town parking (Via Duchessa di Genova)
parking.notes: Paid street parking and small lots along Stresa's lakefront and town centre. Stresa rail station is ~500 m from the main ferry dock. Alternative ferry embarkation from Baveno or Verbania/Pallanza. NO car access whatsoever to the islands.

entrance.name: Isola Bella boat landing (Navigazione Laghi ferry from Stresa)
entrance.transfer: Navigazione Laghi ferry from Stresa main dock or Carciano (~5 min to Isola Bella)

Borromean Islands.getting_there: Drive or train to Stresa; park near the lakefront. Navigazione Laghi ferry to Isola Bella (~5 min). Adult entry: Isola Bella €22, Isola Madre €19, combined €34. No cars on islands.

Brissago Islands.notes: Garden entry only; boat to islands is extra (~CHF 25 return from Locarno).

Byōdō-in Phoenix Hall (Hōō-dō).notes: Garden + Hōshōkan Museum ¥700 adult. Phoenix Hall interior additional ¥300 (separate timed ticket, ~20-min tours). Hours: 08:30–17:30; Hōshōkan 09:00–17:00.
Byōdō-in Phoenix Hall (Hōō-dō).getting_there: JR Nara Line to JR Uji Stn (10-min walk) or Keihan Uji Line to Keihan Uji Stn (10-min walk). ~30 min from Kyoto Stn.

Castles of Bellinzona.notes: Combined ticket for all three castles (Castelgrande, Montebello, Sasso Corbaro).

Caumasee (Lake Cauma).notes: Funicular + bathing area in summer; free outside the lido season.

Centovalli Railway.notes: Locarno–Domodossola one-way; covered by Swiss Travel Pass.

Charles Kuonen Suspension Bridge.notes: Free hike from Randa or Europaweg.

Chichu Art Museum.notes: Adult ¥2,500 online weekday / ¥2,700 weekend; on-site +¥300. Reservation required. Hours: 10:00–18:00 (Mar–Sep) / 10:00–17:00 (Oct–Feb), closed Mon.
Chichu Art Museum.getting_there: Ferry Uno→Miyanoura (~20 min) or Takamatsu→Miyanoura (~60 min), then town bus to Tsutsujiso, then museum shuttle. No private cars to museum.

parking.name: Parking Château de Chillon — north of castle (Av. de Chillon 21)
parking.notes: Dedicated visitor lot north of the island fortress, signed from the N9 / Route Cantonale lakeside road between Montreux (3 km NW) and Villeneuve (2 km SE). 50 m to castle bridge. Walkable from Montreux (45 min lakeside) or regional train to Veytaux-Chillon (4 min from Montreux). Castle admission CHF 14 (2024).

entrance.name: Château de Chillon — main entrance bridge
entrance.transfer: ~50 m walk from north car park across the access bridge onto the island fortress

Chillon Castle (Château de Chillon).getting_there: Drive to Château de Chillon on Lake Geneva; park in the castle's own lot north of the fortress (free est., Av. de Chillon 21, Veytaux); 50 m walk to the medieval island castle entrance.

Chūreitō Pagoda — Arakurayama Sengen Par.notes: Free public park & pagoda viewpoint. 398-step climb (or ramp). Hours: 24h open; cherry blossom season (early-mid April) extremely crowded. Free parking with paid coin-lot overflow in season.
Chūreitō Pagoda — Arakurayama Sengen Par.getting_there: Fujikyū Railway to Shimo-yoshida Stn (10-min walk to park base, then 398 steps). From Tokyo: JR Chūō to Otsuki, transfer to Fujikyū.

Chūson-ji & Konjiki-dō.notes: ¥1,000 adult combined: Konjiki-dō + Sankōzō (Treasure Hall) + Kyōzō + Kyū-Ōidō. Hours: 08:30–17:00 (Mar 1–Nov 3), 08:30–16:30 (Nov 4–Feb). Open year-round.
Chūson-ji & Konjiki-dō.getting_there: JR Tōhoku Shinkansen to Ichinoseki, transfer to JR Tōhoku Main Line to Hiraizumi (~8 min), then loop bus 'Run Run' or 25-min walk.

Crans-Montana.notes: Resort is free to walk; lifts and ski passes are extra.

parking.name: Talstation Dachstein-Krippenstein-Seilbahn (Obertraun)
parking.notes: Valley station in Obertraun village (~511 m) on the SE shore of Lake Hallstatt. Free parking at/near the valley station. A1 Regau exit → B145; train station 'Dachsteinhöhlen' on the Salzkammergutbahn, then bus to valley station.

entrance.name: Dachstein-Krippenstein-Seilbahn valley station (Obertraun)
entrance.transfer: Two-stage cable car (Obertraun ~511 m → Schönbergalm → Krippenstein plateau 2,108 m), ~15 min total; 5fingers and Dachstein Giant Ice Cave from plateau

Dachstein Krippenstein & 5fingers Platfo.getting_there: Drive to Obertraun (SE Lake Hallstatt, B166); park at Krippenstein cable-car valley station (est. free). Two-stage cable car (~15 min) to Krippenstein (2,108 m) with 5fingers viewpoint and Dachstein Ice Cave.

parking.name: Talstation Dachstein-Gletscherbahn (Ramsau am Dachstein)
parking.notes: Valley station in Ramsau am Dachstein (Styria), ~1,750 m. Parking appears free per multiple travel sources (not explicitly confirmed on official site). 2026 season: 9 May – 8 Nov, daily 08:00–17:20. Gondola spots MUST be pre-booked online. Ice Palace, suspension bridge, Stairway to Nothingness open 09:00–16:15.

entrance.name: Dachstein Südwandbahn valley station (Talstation Ramsau)
entrance.transfer: Cable car ~8–10 min to glacier plateau (2,700 m); Sky Walk platform adjacent to mountain station

Dachstein Sky Walk & Glacier.getting_there: Drive to Ramsau am Dachstein (Styria); park at valley station (~1,750 m, est. free). Südwandbahn cable car (~8 min) to Sky Walk & glacier at 2,700 m. Mandatory gondola pre-booking online in summer.

Danjō Garan & Konpon Daitō.notes: Outer Danjō Garan precinct free; Konpon Daitō interior ¥500 adult, Kondō ¥500 (combined ticket available). Hours: 08:30–17:00. Kongōbu-ji separate ¥1,000.

parking.name: Chūmon-mae Parking (Danjō Garan)
parking.notes: Public lots around Garan and Kongōbu-ji

Danjō Garan & Konpon Daitō.getting_there: Nankai Kōya Line to Gokurakubashi, then cable car to Kōyasan Stn, transfer to Nankai Rinkan Bus to Kondō-mae stop (~10 min).

Dazaifu Tenmangū & Kōmyōzen-ji.notes: Dazaifu Tenmangū grounds free, 06:00–19:00 approx (varies seasonally). Kōmyōzen-ji garden ¥200 donation, 08:00–17:00. Main hall under reconstruction with temporary hall until 2027.
Dazaifu Tenmangū & Kōmyōzen-ji.getting_there: Nishitetsu Tenjin-Ōmuta Line to Nishitetsu-Futsukaichi, transfer to Dazaifu Line to Dazaifu Stn; 5-min walk via shopping street.

Diavolezza.notes: Cable car return from Bernina Diavolezza station.

Dōtonbori.notes: Free public entertainment district along Dōtonbori canal. Open 24h; most shops/restaurants 11:00–23:00 or later. Iconic Glico sign, Dotonbori Riverwalk.
Dōtonbori.getting_there: Osaka Metro Midōsuji/Sennichimae/Yotsubashi Lines to Namba Stn (3-min walk) or Nippombashi Stn. From Shin-Osaka: Midōsuji Line ~9 min.

Ebenalp & Berggasthaus Äscher.notes: Wasserauen–Ebenalp cable car return; gasthaus payment separate.

parking.name: Wasserauen parking (Ebenalp cable car base)
parking.notes: Coin-operated (CHF/EUR coins) or local parking app. 2-min walk from Wasserauen railway station (Appenzeller Bahnen S23).

entrance.name: Wasserauen – Ebenalp cable car valley station
entrance.transfer: Cable car (every 15 min, ~5 min) to Ebenalp (1,640 m), then 15-min hike through Wildkirchli cave to Berggasthaus Äscher

Ebenalp & Berggasthaus Äscher.getting_there: Park at Wasserauen; 5-min cable car to Ebenalp (1,640 m); hike 15 min through the ancient Wildkirchli cave to iconic Berggasthaus Äscher — Switzerland's most-photographed cliffside inn.

Eihei-ji.notes: Adult ¥700 (jr/sr-high ¥500, elementary ¥200). Hours: 08:30–16:30 (last entry 16:00). Active Sōtō Zen monastery — modest dress required; remain on wooden corridors.
Eihei-ji.getting_there: Hokuriku Shinkansen to Fukui Stn, then Keifuku bus 'Eiheiji Liner' direct (~30 min) or Echizen Railway to Eiheijiguchi + bus.

Eisriesenwelt Ice Cave.notes: Combi ticket (cable car + cave) €45 cash / €41 online ≈ CHF 43.

parking.name: Eisriesenwelt Visitor Centre Car Park (~1,600 m)
parking.notes: Reached via a 5 km mountain road (toll-free) from Werfen — gradient up to 21%. Caravans prohibited; motorhomes not recommended. Shuttle bus from Werfen station available in peak season.

entrance.name: Eisriesenwelt Cave Entrance (1,656 m)
entrance.transfer: 3-min cable car from visitor centre + 15–20-min alpine trail to cave entrance

Eisriesenwelt Ice Cave.getting_there: Drive to Werfen, then 5 km up the mountain road to the visitor centre (free parking). 3-min cable car + 15-min walk to the cave. Combi ticket €41 online / €45 cash. Open May 1 – Oct 26.

Emmentaler Schaukäserei (Affoltern).notes: Show dairy entry is free; restaurant and shop separate.

Engelberg Abbey.notes: Church free; guided tours of the abbey CHF 12.

Enryaku-ji (Hiei-zan).notes: Combined ticket (Tōdō+Saitō+Yokawa) ¥1,000 adult; Kokuhō-den +¥500. Hours: Tōdō 08:30–16:30 (Mar–Nov), 09:00–16:00 (Dec–Feb); Saitō/Yokawa close 30 min earlier.

parking.name: Enryaku-ji Tōdō parking
parking.notes: Free lots at Tōdō, Saitō and Yokawa; toll required on Hiei Driveway.

Enryaku-ji (Hiei-zan).getting_there: From Kyoto: Keihan/Eizan to Yase-Hieizan-guchi, then Eizan Cable + Ropeway. From Sakamoto-side: Sakamoto Cable Car from Cable Sakamoto Stn (~11 min).

First Cliff Walk, Grindelwald.notes: Grindelwald–First gondola return; cliff walk and Bachalpsee included free.

Fronalpstock / Stoos.notes: Stoosbahn funicular + Fronalpstock chairlift combined return.

Fukui Prefectural Dinosaur Museum.notes: Permanent exhibit ¥1,000 adult; with special exhibit ¥1,800 (Jul 10–Nov 3 2026 Sauropods theme). Timed-entry advance booking required (60 days ahead). Hours: 09:00–17:00; extended 08:30–17:30 Jul 18–Aug 16.
Fukui Prefectural Dinosaur Museum.getting_there: Hokuriku Shinkansen to Fukui, transfer to Echizen Railway Katsuyama Eiheiji Line to Katsuyama Stn (~55 min), then community bus (~15 min).

Fushimi Inari Taisha.notes: Shrine grounds & torii paths free. Hours: open 24 h, main hall lit until ~late evening. Mountain hike to summit ~2–3 h round-trip.

parking.name: Fushimi Inari Taisha free parking
parking.notes: Free shrine lot; very limited, closes during festivals (Jan 1–5). Use Kyoto-bound trains during peak.

Fushimi Inari Taisha.getting_there: 5 min walk from JR Inari Stn (Nara Line, 5 min from Kyoto Stn); 7 min walk from Fushimi-Inari Stn (Keihan Main Line).

Ghibli Museum.notes: Adult ¥1,000 (13-18: ¥700, 7-12: ¥400, 4-6: ¥100). Advance reservation ONLY via Lawson Ticket — released 10th of prior month 10:00 JST. No walk-ups. Hours: 10:00–18:00, closed Tue. Timed entry slots.
Ghibli Museum.getting_there: JR Chūō Line to Mitaka Stn (15-min walk through Inokashira Park) or community 'Mitaka City Bus' Ghibli Museum shuttle (5 min). No on-site parking.

Ghibli Park.notes: O-Sanpo Day Pass Premium (all 5 areas) ¥7,300 weekday/¥7,800 weekend, adult. Cheaper single-area passes also sold. Advance reservation only via Lawson Ticket / Klook; timed entry for Grand Warehouse. Hours: 09:00–17:00 (varies). Closed Tue.
Ghibli Park.getting_there: Linimo (Tobu Kyūryō Line) to Ai-Chikyūhaku-Kinen-Kōen Stn; park entrance adjacent. From Nagoya: Higashiyama Line to Fujigaoka then Linimo (~35 min total).

Ginkaku-ji (Silver Pavilion).notes: Adult/HS ¥500, ES/JHS ¥300. Hours: 08:30–17:00 (Mar–Nov), 09:00–16:30 (Dec–Feb). Famous moss & sand gardens; Ashikaga Yoshimasa's retirement villa.

parking.name: Kyoto Municipal Ginkakuji Parking
parking.notes: 3-min walk from gate

Ginkaku-ji (Silver Pavilion).getting_there: Kyoto City Bus 5/17/100/203 from Kyoto Stn to 'Ginkakuji-michi' (~35 min), 10-min walk uphill via Philosopher's Path approach.

Glacier 3000 / Diablerets Peak Walk.notes: Col du Pillon return; Peak Walk and Ice Express included.

parking.name: Parking Village — La Palud-sur-Verdon (D952)
parking.notes: Free open-air village parking at the main gateway hub for the gorge. Tourist office + cafés + trailhead access. Drive D23 Route des Crêtes east to viewpoints. Peak summer weekends may see overflow meadow parking ~€2–3/vehicle.

entrance.name: Belvédère du Trescaire — north-rim viewpoint (D23, Route des Crêtes)
entrance.transfer: ~5 km drive east of La Palud along D23 to roadside layby

Gorges du Verdon.getting_there: From Castellane: D952 west (~25 km) to La Palud-sur-Verdon (free village parking); D23 Route des Crêtes east along the north rim to Belvédère du Trescaire and other belvederes.

parking.name: Matterhorn Terminal Täsch (P+R)
parking.notes: Same covered garage as Klein Matterhorn (Zermatt is car-free). Open-air lots in Täsch ~CHF 8/day. MGB shuttle train every 20 min, 12-min ride to Zermatt; GGB cogwheel station is inside Zermatt, ~5-min walk from the shuttle arrival platform.

entrance.name: Zermatt — Gornergratbahn (GGB) cogwheel station (1,605 m)
entrance.transfer: Täsch → Zermatt shuttle (12 min) + 5-min walk to GGB; cogwheel 33 min to Gornergrat (3,089 m)

Gornergrat.getting_there: Park Matterhorn Terminal Täsch (CHF 16/day, 2,100 covered spaces); 12-min shuttle to car-free Zermatt; 5-min walk to Gornergratbahn; 33-min cogwheel to Gornergrat (3,089 m) for the Matterhorn panorama.

Goryōkaku.notes: Star-shaped park free, open 05:00–19:00 (Apr–Oct) / 05:00–18:00 (Nov–Mar). Magistrate's Office (Bugyōsho) ¥500 adult; Goryōkaku Tower ¥1,000 (separate).

parking.name: Goryōkaku Tower / Hakodate City parking
parking.notes: Multiple paid coin lots ring the park; tower lot is closest to entrance.

Goryōkaku.getting_there: Hakodate City Tram to Goryōkaku-Kōen-mae stop (~17 min from Hakodate Stn), then 10 min walk. By car: 10 min from Hakodate Stn.

Grimentz.notes: Village free; cable car to Bendolla CHF 27 return.

Gruyères Village & Castle.notes: Castle entry; village walking is free.

Gunkanjima / Hashima Island.notes: Gunkanjima Concierge Standard tour ¥5,500 weekday / ¥6,000 peak adult + ¥310 island landing fee. Landing tour only (no individual access). Departures 10:30 & 13:40 from Tokiwa Pier. Includes Digital Museum.
Gunkanjima / Hashima Island.getting_there: Nagasaki Tram Line 1 to Ōura-Tenshudō or Shinchi-Chūkagai. From JR Nagasaki Stn ~15 min by tram/taxi to Tokiwa Pier.

Habsburg Castle.notes: Outdoor castle area free; restaurant separate.

Haguro-san — Five-Storey Pagoda & 2446 S.notes: Free open-air pilgrimage. 5-storey pagoda (~10 min walk from Zuishinmon gate) viewable year-round 24h. 2,446 stone steps continue to summit shrine. Pagoda interior special openings ¥500 (limited dates).

parking.name: Zuishinmon trailhead
parking.notes: Paid summit lot ¥400

Haguro-san — Five-Storey Pagoda & 2446 S.getting_there: JR Uetsu Line to Tsuruoka Stn, then Shōnai Kōtsū bus to 'Zuishinmon' or 'Haguro Center' (~35 min). From summit, bus continues to Mt Haguro top.

Hakkōda Ropeway.notes: Round-trip adult ¥2,200, child ¥700 (one-way ¥1,400). Hours: 09:00–16:20. OPERATIONS SUSPENDED May 7–Jun 13 2026 for wire-rope replacement. Famous winter snow-monsters (juhyō).
Hakkōda Ropeway.getting_there: JR Aomori Stn → JR Bus Tōhoku 'Mizuumi-go' to Hakkōda Ropeway-mae (~65 min). By car: ~30 min from Aomori-Chūō IC via Rt 103.

Hakone Open-Air Museum.notes: Open-air sculpture museum with ~120 modern/contemporary works on 70,000 m²; Picasso Pavilion included in general ticket. Hours 09:00–17:00 (last entry 16:30).
Hakone Open-Air Museum.getting_there: From Hakone-Yumoto, take Hakone Tozan Railway to Chōkoku-no-Mori Station (~40 min) and walk 2 minutes.

Hakone Shrine & Heiwa-no-Torii.notes: Shrine grounds and lakeside Heiwa-no-Torii ('Torii of Peace') free; Treasure Hall ¥500. Open 24h; Treasure Hall 09:00–16:00.

parking.name: Free shrine parking
parking.notes: Fills early; paid lots at Moto-Hakone ~¥500/h

Hakone Shrine & Heiwa-no-Torii.getting_there: Hakone Tozan Bus from Hakone-Yumoto to Moto-Hakone-ko stop (~40 min), then 10 min walk.

Hakone-Yumoto Onsen.notes: Onsen town itself is free to wander; individual baths/ryokan charge ¥1,000–¥2,000 day-use. Hours: town open 24 h; shops 09:00–18:00 typical.

parking.name: Hakone-Yumoto town parking
parking.notes: Several paid coin/town lots near the station and along the Sukumo River.

Hakone-Yumoto Onsen.getting_there: Odakyu Romancecar from Shinjuku to Hakone-Yumoto Stn (~85 min) or change at Odawara. By car: 10 min from Hakone-guchi IC (Odawara-Atsugi Rd).

Hakuba Happō-One.notes: Summer green-season Alpen Line round-trip (gondola + 2 lifts) ~¥3,800 adult, reaches Happō-ike pond (1,830 m). Operational late-Jun to early-Nov; winter ski resort (separate lift tickets).

parking.name: Happo base lots
parking.notes: Paid ¥500-1000 in winter

Hakuba Happō-One.getting_there: From Nagano Stn ~70 min by Alpico express bus to Happo bus terminal.

parking.name: Parkplatz P1 – Hallstatt Ortsende (near Kalvarienbergkirche)
parking.notes: P1 is year-round and the only lot with a shuttle bus to hotels in the centre. P2 (near Salzbergseilbahn, seasonal) is closer to Salzwelten. Historic centre is sealed by automatic barriers — no through-traffic.

entrance.name: Hallstatt Marktplatz (village centre)
entrance.transfer: 20-min walk from P1; or shuttle bus from P1 to accommodation (daily 09:00–19:00, year-round)

Hallstatt.getting_there: Drive to gated P1/P2 at the village edge (centre is car-free); walk ~20 min or take the shuttle from P1. Centre is sealed by automatic barriers.

parking.name: Parkplatz P1 Hallstatt (near Kalvarienbergkirche)
parking.notes: Year-round, automated barriers; pay at kiosk (cash, Maestro, Visa, Mastercard). Village centre is barriered off — no through access. P2 (near Salzwelten) open spring–autumn only; Salzwelten + skywalk currently closed for reconstruction until summer 2026. No advance booking for day visitors.

entrance.name: Hallstatt Marktplatz — lakeshore promenade (Seestraße)
entrance.transfer: ~20-min walk north along road from P1; shuttle bus P1 → hotels for overnight guests only

Hallstätter See (Lake Hallstatt).getting_there: Park at P1 (€20/day) outside the gated village; 20-min walk to lakeshore promenade. Day spots fill fast and cannot be pre-booked.

Hammetschwand Elevator / Bürgenstock.notes: Hammetschwand elevator return; Bürgenstock funicular extra.

Hase-dera.notes: Admission ¥400 adult; famous 9.18 m gilded eleven-headed Kannon, hydrangea path (Jun) and ocean view. Hours 08:00–16:30 (Apr–Jun until 17:00).
Hase-dera.getting_there: Enoden Line, Hase Station, 5 min walk. From Kamakura Stn ~5 min by Enoden.

Higashi Chaya District.notes: Historic geisha teahouse district; streetscape free to walk 24h. Interior of Shima Teahouse ¥500, Kaikaro ¥750, gold-leaf Hakuza free.
Higashi Chaya District.getting_there: Kanazawa Loop Bus (RL/LL) from JR Kanazawa Stn to Hashibachō stop (~15 min), then 5 min walk.

Himeji Castle (Himeji-jō).notes: New fee from 2026-03-01: adult (non-resident) ¥2,500; Himeji-citizen ¥1,000; combined w/ Kōko-en ¥2,600; under-18 free. Hours: 09:00–17:00 (last entry 16:00); to 18:00 in summer.

parking.name: Ōtemon Parking (Himeji city lot)
parking.notes: Closest public lot south of Ōtemon gate, ~5 min walk to castle.

Himeji Castle (Himeji-jō).getting_there: 20 min walk N from JR/Sanyō Himeji Stn, or Shinki bus to Ōtemon-mae (5 min). Shinkansen: ~30 min from Shin-Ōsaka, ~20 min from Okayama.

parking.name: Talstation Hintertux — underground car park
parking.notes: Underground car park at the valley station (1,500 m, end of Tuxertal, Zillertal). Barrier-free access to glacier cable cars. Paid parking; exact rate not publicly listed. Road dead-ends here — no alternative parking.

entrance.name: Hintertux Gletscherbahn valley station (1,500 m)
entrance.transfer: Gletscherbus 1 from valley station to Sommerbergalm (2,100 m); Gletscherbus 2/3 to 3,250 m; total ~20 min. Year-round.

Hintertuxer Gletscher.getting_there: Drive to end of Tuxertal (B173, Zillertal); park in underground garage at Hintertux valley station (1,500 m, ~€5–6/day est.). Gletscherbus cable cars rise to 3,250 m. Year-round glacier; book tickets online.

Hirosaki Castle.notes: Honmaru + Kita-no-Kuruwa admission ¥320 adult; combined (incl. Botanical Garden & Fujita Memorial Garden) ¥520. Park itself free. Hours: 09:00–17:00 (Apr 1–Nov 23); paid area closed in winter.

parking.name: Hirosaki Park Tsuru-no-mai parking
parking.notes: Several city-run lots ring the park; expect peak pricing during Cherry Blossom Festival.

Hirosaki Castle.getting_there: 15 min by 100-yen Dotemachi loop bus from JR Hirosaki Stn to Shiyakusho-mae, then 5 min walk. By car: 30 min from Ōwani-Hirosaki IC.

Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park & A-Bomb D.notes: Park & A-Bomb Dome free, open 24 h. Peace Memorial Museum ¥200 adult, 08:30–18:00 (to 19:00 Aug; to 17:00 Dec–Feb), closed Dec 30–31.

parking.name: Peace Memorial Park underground parking
parking.notes: Underground lot directly south of museum entrance.

Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park & A-Bomb D.getting_there: Hiroden tram #2/#6 from Hiroshima Stn to Genbaku-Dome-mae (~15 min). Shinkansen Nozomi: ~1h25 from Shin-Ōsaka.

Hitachi Seaside Park.notes: Adult ¥450; famous for ~4.5 million nemophila blue flowers in Apr–May, red kochia in Oct. Hours 09:30–17:00.
Hitachi Seaside Park.getting_there: JR Joban Line to Katsuta Stn, then Ibaraki Kotsu bus ~15 min to 'Kaihin-koen Nishi-guchi'.

parking.name: Altstadt Garage / Parkgarage Mönchsberg (Salzburg Old Town)
parking.notes: Salzburg Altstadt is a pedestrian zone — no car access to the fortress hill. Nearest garages: Altstadt (under Mönchsberg cliff) and Mirabell. Walk 10–20 min to Festungsgasse funicular base.

entrance.name: Festungsbahn Lower Station – Festungsgasse (Salzburg)
entrance.transfer: 1-min funicular (every 10 min, 55 pax/car); or 30-min footpath up Festungsberg

Hohensalzburg Fortress.getting_there: Park in an Altstadt garage (old town is pedestrian-only); walk 10–20 min to Festungsgasse; take the Festungsbahn funicular (1 min) to the fortress. All-inclusive ticket €19.20.

Hōryū-ji.notes: Combined ticket (Saiin + Daihōzōin + Tōin) ¥1,500 adult. Hours: 08:00–17:00 (Feb 22–Nov 3) / 08:00–16:30 (Nov 4–Feb 21). Note: some sources cite a 2025 revision to ¥2,000.

parking.name: Hōryū-ji iChi-no-Torii paid parking
parking.notes: Coin lots along the sandō approach 200 m east of Nandaimon.

Hōryū-ji.getting_there: From JR Hōryūji Stn (Yamatoji Line, 11 min from Nara): 20 min walk or local bus to Hōryūji-mae (8 min). By car: 5 min from Hōryūji IC (Nishi-Meihan Expwy).

Imperial Palace East Garden & Nijūbashi.notes: East Gardens free, no ticket; numbered entry token returned at exit. Hours: 09:00–17:00 (Mar–Apr 14 & Sep), 09:00–18:00 (Apr 15–Aug), 09:00–16:30 (Nov–Feb). Closed Mon & Fri. Nijūbashi (outer plaza) free 24 h.
Imperial Palace East Garden & Nijūbashi.getting_there: 5 min walk from Otemachi Stn (Marunouchi/Chiyoda/Tōzai/Mita/Hanzōmon lines) to Ōtemon Gate; 10 min from Tokyo Stn Marunouchi Exit.

Ine no Funaya (Boathouse Village).notes: Village free to walk; ~230 funaya (boathouses) line the bay. Sightseeing boat ¥1,200 adult (25 min, hourly 09:00–16:00).
Ine no Funaya (Boathouse Village).getting_there: From Amanohashidate Stn, Tankai Bus to Ine ~55 min (¥400). By car ~1 h from Miyazu via Rte 178.

Ise Jingū — Naikū & Gekū.notes: Both Naikū (Inner) and Gekū (Outer) shrines free, no ticket. Hours: 05:00–18:00 (May–Aug); 05:00–17:00 (Oct–Dec); 05:00–18:00 (Jan–Apr & Sep); open 365 d.

parking.name: Naikū A/B free parking
parking.notes: Free Naikū lots (A1/A2/B); paid lots (¥500/day) when free lots are full, esp. weekends.

Ise Jingū — Naikū & Gekū.getting_there: Bus from Ise-shi Stn (JR/Kintetsu) to Naikū-mae (~20 min, ¥460); Gekū is 5 min walk from Ise-shi Stn. Shinkansen via Nagoya to Ise via Kintetsu.

Iseltwald.notes: Village free; the famous private pier charges CHF 5 for photos.

Itsukushima Shrine & Floating Torii.notes: Shrine ¥300 adult; combined ticket w/ Treasure Hall ¥500. Hours: 06:30–18:00 (Mar–Oct 14), 06:30–17:30 (Oct 15–Nov / Jan–Feb), 06:30–17:00 (Dec). Also ¥100 Miyajima visitor tax.
Itsukushima Shrine & Floating Torii.getting_there: JR ferry (10 min, JR Pass valid) or Matsudai ferry from Miyajimaguchi (~3 min walk from JR Miyajimaguchi Stn, San-yō Line) to Miyajima pier; 10 min walk to shrine. No cars on island.

Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine.notes: UNESCO WHS. Ryūgenji Mabu mine shaft entry ¥410 adult (09:00–17:00). Ōmori townscape free. Guided Ōkubo Mabu tour (advance booking) ~¥4,500.

parking.name: Sekai-Isan Centre P&R
parking.notes: Shuttle bus ¥200 into Ōmori

Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine.getting_there: JR Sanin Line to Ōda-shi Stn, then Iwami-Kotsu bus to 'Ōmori' ~30 min.

Izumo Taisha.notes: Grounds free, no ticket. Hours: 06:00–20:00 (Mar–Oct) / 06:30–20:00 (Nov–Feb). Treasure Hall (Homotsuden) ¥300.

parking.name: Izumo Taisha free parking
parking.notes: Free shrine lots N and E of grounds; full on weekends/New Year.

Izumo Taisha.getting_there: Ichibata Railway to Izumo-Taisha-mae Stn (~25 min from Matsue-Shinjiko-Onsen), 5 min walk. Or JR Izumoshi Stn + Ichibata bus (~25 min).

Jigokudani Monkey Park.notes: Adult ¥800, child ¥400. Hours: 08:30–17:00 (Apr–Oct), 09:00–16:00 (Nov–Mar). 30–40 min walk on uneven snowy trail from parking; not stroller-friendly.

parking.name: Kanbayashi Onsen / Snow Monkey parking
parking.notes: Trailhead lot; from here ~1.6 km forest walk (~30 min) to park entrance.

Jigokudani Monkey Park.getting_there: Nagano Dentetsu ltd-express from Nagano Stn to Yudanaka (~45 min), then Nagaden bus to Kanbayashi Onsen (~15 min), then 30 min walk. By car: 15 min from Shinshū-Nakano IC.

Jungfraujoch – Top of Europe.notes: Interlaken Ost return; cheaper Good Morning ticket and seasonal discounts available.

parking.name: Grindelwald Terminal car park
parking.notes: 1,032-space multi-storey at Grindelwald Terminal (Grundstrasse 54). Eiger Express departure hub. Alternative: Lauterbrunnen village (~CHF 5–8/day) + Wengernalp Railway + Jungfrau Railway.

entrance.name: Grindelwald Terminal – Eiger Express tricable gondola
entrance.transfer: Eiger Express gondola to Eigergletscher (15 min), then Jungfrau Railway cogwheel through Eiger–Mönch tunnel to Jungfraujoch (30 min)

Jungfraujoch – Top of Europe.getting_there: Park at Grindelwald Terminal (1,032 spaces); board the Eiger Express gondola (15 min) to Eigergletscher; transfer to the Jungfrau cogwheel railway for the 30-min tunnel climb.

Jōmon Sugi.notes: Trail itself free but Yakushima Recreational Forest entry ¥1000/day. Realistic access requires guide (¥15,000–25,000) + Arakawa shuttle bus (¥1,740 RT, mandatory Mar–Nov). ~10hr round-trip hike. Hours: trail open dawn–dusk.
Jōmon Sugi.getting_there: From Yakushima port: drive/taxi to Yakusugi Shizen-kan, then mandatory Arakawa shuttle bus (~35 min) to Arakawa trailhead; private cars banned Mar–Nov.

Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe.notes: Großglockner High Alpine Road car day toll €46.50 (2026) ≈ CHF 44. After 18:00 €36.50; E-car €40. Road open late Apr – late Oct.

parking.name: Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe Summit Car Park (2,369 m)
parking.notes: Toll booths: Ferleiten (Salzburg/N) or Heiligenblut (Carinthia/S). Parking is free once the toll is paid. The parking IS the destination — no further transfer needed.

entrance.name: Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe Visitor Centre & Glacier Overlook (2,369 m)
entrance.transfer: Drive directly — parking at destination

Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe.getting_there: Pay €46.50 toll at Ferleiten (N) or Heiligenblut (S). Drive the 48 km scenic Großglockner High Alpine Road to the summit car park. Open late Apr – late Oct.

Kasamatsu Park ('mata-nozoki' viewpoint).notes: Park free, but reached only via chairlift/cable car ¥800 round-trip adult. Famous 'mata-nozoki' (between-the-legs) view. Hours 09:00–17:00.
Kasamatsu Park ('mata-nozoki' viewpoint).getting_there: Tankai bus or boat (¥800) across the bay from Amanohashidate Stn to Ichinomiya, then cable car/chairlift (4 min).

Kasuga Taisha.notes: Outer grounds and ~3,000 stone/bronze lanterns free; inner sanctuary 'special worship' ¥700, Treasure Hall ¥500, Manyō Botanical Garden ¥500. Hours 06:30–17:30 (winter from 07:00).
Kasuga Taisha.getting_there: Nara Kotsu bus from JR/Kintetsu Nara Stn to 'Kasuga-Taisha Honden' (~10 min), or 25 min walk via Nara Park.

Kegon Falls.notes: Upper viewing deck free; elevator (100 m down) to base observation deck ¥570 adult. 97 m drop. Hours 08:00–17:00 (winter 09:00–16:30).
Kegon Falls.getting_there: Tobu bus from Nikko Stn to 'Chūzenji Onsen' ~45 min (¥1,250), 5 min walk.

Kenroku-en.notes: Adult ¥320 (Ishikawa Pref. official). Hours: 07:00–18:00 (Mar 1–Oct 15); 08:00–17:00 (Oct 16–end Feb). Free admission early-morning hours and during certain seasonal events.
Kenroku-en.getting_there: Kenrokuen-shita bus stop, ~15 min by Kanazawa Loop Bus from Kanazawa Stn (¥200).

Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion).notes: Adult ¥500 (junior/elementary ¥300). Hours: 09:00–17:00 daily, no closing day. Official site confirms 9–17 access.
Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion).getting_there: Kyoto City Bus #101/205 from Kyoto Stn to Kinkakuji-michi (~40 min, ¥230); 3 min walk.

parking.name: Gletscherjet 1 / Panoramabahn valley station — Kaprun
parking.notes: FREE parking confirmed on official kitzsteinhorn.at arrivals page. EV charging stations on site. GPS confirmed by operator. Address: Kesselfallstraße 60, 5710 Kaprun. Second valley station (MK Maiskogelbahn/3K K-onnection) at Kitzsteinhornplatz 1a also has free parking but is not open year-round. Postbus 660 from Zell am See serves the valley station.

entrance.name: Kitzsteinhorn Gletscherjet 1 cable-car valley station (Kaprun, 911 m)
entrance.transfer: Gletscherjet 1 funitel + Panoramabahn to Alpincenter (2,450 m), then further lifts to 3,029 m Top of Salzburg. Open year-round.

Kitzsteinhorn Glacier (Top of Salzburg).getting_there: Drive to Kaprun (Salzburg, A10 → B311); FREE parking + EV charging at Gletscherjet 1 valley station (911 m). Cable cars to 3,029 m Top of Salzburg. Open year-round; Postbus 660 from Zell am See also serves.

Kiyomizu-dera + Sannenzaka/Ninenzaka.notes: Main Hall adult ¥500 (jr/elem ¥200). Sannenzaka/Ninenzaka lanes are free public streets. Hours: 06:00–18:00 (extended to 18:30 summer; special evening viewings ¥500 spring/autumn).
Kiyomizu-dera + Sannenzaka/Ninenzaka.getting_there: City Bus 100/206 from Kyoto Stn to Gojo-zaka or Kiyomizu-michi (~15 min); ~10 min uphill walk.

parking.name: Matterhorn Terminal Täsch (P+R)
parking.notes: Covered multi-storey garage. Required because Zermatt is car-free. Open-air lots also available in Täsch at lower rates (~CHF 8/day). Pre-book in peak season.

entrance.name: Zermatt – Matterhorn Glacier Paradise valley station
entrance.transfer: Shuttle train Täsch → Zermatt every 20 min (12 min) + ~10-min walk to cable-car valley station

Klein Matterhorn – Matterhorn Glacier Pa.getting_there: Park Matterhorn Terminal Täsch (CHF 16/day, 2,100 covered spaces); 12-min shuttle train to car-free Zermatt; 10-min walk to Matterhorn Glacier Paradise cable car.

Kongōbu-ji.notes: Head temple of Kōyasan Shingon Buddhism. ¥1,000 (08:30–17:00); includes Banryūtei rock garden — Japan's largest. Combined ticket with Garan/Reihōkan ~¥2,500.

parking.name: Temple lot
parking.notes: Very limited

Kongōbu-ji.getting_there: Nankai Kōya Line from Osaka Namba to Gokurakubashi, then cable car to Kōyasan Stn (~5 min), then Nankai Rinkan bus to 'Kongōbu-ji-mae' (~10 min).

Konponchūdō (Enryaku-ji innermost hall).notes: National Treasure innermost hall of Enryaku-ji complex; access via combined Tōtō+Saitō+Yokawa ticket ¥1,000 adult. Multi-year roof restoration. Hours 08:30–16:30.

parking.name: Enryaku-ji Tōtō lot
parking.notes: Hiei-zan Driveway toll ¥1,700 each way

Konponchūdō (Enryaku-ji innermost hall).getting_there: From Kyoto Stn: JR/Keihan to Hiei-zan Sakamoto, Sakamoto Cable Car to Cable Enryaku-ji Stn, 8 min walk.

Kotohira-gū (Konpira-san).notes: Famed for 785 stone steps to main shrine (Honkū), 1,368 steps total to Okusha. Shrine grounds free. Treasure Hall ¥800, Shoin ¥800. Palanquin chair partway ~¥6,800.
Kotohira-gū (Konpira-san).getting_there: JR Dosan Line / Kotoden to Kotohira Stn, 15 min walk to base of steps. From Takamatsu ~1 h.

Kumamoto Castle.notes: Tenshu reopened Apr 2021 after 2016 quake; yagura/stone-wall reconstruction ongoing. Adult ¥800 (combined w/ Wakuwakuza ¥1,100). Hours 09:00–17:00.
Kumamoto Castle.getting_there: Kumamoto City Tram from JR Kumamoto Stn to 'Kumamoto-jō-mae' (~17 min, ¥180), 5 min walk.

Kumano Hongū Taisha & Ōyunohara torii.notes: Shrine grounds and giant 33.9 m Ōyunohara torii (Japan's largest) free. Hōmotsuden treasure hall ¥300. Hours ~08:00–17:00.
Kumano Hongū Taisha & Ōyunohara torii.getting_there: Ryūjin / Meikō bus from JR Kii-Tanabe Stn (~2 h, ¥2,100) to 'Hongū Taisha-mae'.

Kurobe Dam.notes: Access only via Tateyama Kurobe Alpine Route, ~15-Apr to 30-Nov. Round-trip Ōgizawa↔Murodō ¥10,940; through-traverse ¥13,170. Dam free to walk. Discharge late-Jun to mid-Oct.

parking.name: Ōgizawa or Tateyama Stn
parking.notes: Through-traverse can't return to car

Kurobe Dam.getting_there: Eastern: JR Shinano-Ōmachi Stn → Alpico bus to Ōgizawa (~40 min) → Kanden Electric Bus 16 min to Kurobe Dam.

Kōfuku-ji & Five-Storey Pagoda.notes: Grounds and 50.1 m Five-Storey Pagoda (Japan's 2nd tallest) free 24h. Pagoda currently under roof restoration into ~2031. Central Golden Hall ¥500, NT Hall ¥700, Eastern Golden Hall ¥300; combined ¥900.
Kōfuku-ji & Five-Storey Pagoda.getting_there: 5 min walk east of Kintetsu Nara Stn; 20 min walk from JR Nara Stn.

Kōraku-en Garden, Okayama.notes: One of Japan's 'three great gardens'. Adult ¥500; combined with Okayama Castle ¥720. Hours 07:30–18:00 (20-Mar–30-Sep), 08:00–17:00 (1-Oct–19-Mar).
Kōraku-en Garden, Okayama.getting_there: Okayama tram from JR Okayama Stn to 'Shiroshita' (~5 min, ¥120), 10 min walk.

Kōtoku-in (Kamakura Daibutsu).notes: Adult ¥300 (jr/elem ¥150); +¥50 to enter inside the Great Buddha statue. Hours: 08:00–17:30 (Apr–Sep), 08:00–17:00 (Oct–Mar); last entry 15 min prior.
Kōtoku-in (Kamakura Daibutsu).getting_there: 7 min walk from Hase Stn (Enoden Line); Enoden runs from JR Kamakura Stn (4 min).

Kōyasan Okunoin.notes: Cemetery/approach free 24h; Torodo Hall (Lantern Hall) 06:00–17:30 free entry. Gokusho offering rite 06:00 & 10:30. Some other Koyasan temples (Kongobu-ji, Danjo Garan) are paid ¥1000 combo.
Kōyasan Okunoin.getting_there: Nankai Koya Line to Gokurakubashi Stn, then cable car to Koyasan Stn, then Nankai Rinkan Bus to Okunoin-mae (~15 min).

La Chaux-de-Fonds — UNESCO Watchmaking C.notes: Old town walking is free; International Watchmaking Museum CHF 15.

parking.name: Parking Plage — Sainte-Croix-du-Verdon village
parking.notes: Seasonal paid parking (Jul–Aug, est.); may be free in shoulder season. Small village lot above the lake shore; kayak and SUP rentals at the beach below. Better-equipped kayak hub is at Pont du Galetas (~12 km east) — also paid in summer.

entrance.name: Plage de Sainte-Croix-du-Verdon — village beach & kayak launch
entrance.transfer: 3-min walk downhill from parking

Lac de Sainte-Croix.getting_there: From Moustiers-Sainte-Marie: D952/D111 (~8 km) to Sainte-Croix-du-Verdon. Seasonal paid parking near beach (~€5/day est.); kayaks at the lakeside. Larger hub: Pont du Galetas.

Lago di Braies (Pragser Wildsee).notes: P4 parking €38/day (2023 ref) ≈ CHF 36. Includes a meal voucher.

parking.name: Parcheggio P4 Lago di Braies
parking.notes: Online booking MANDATORY in peak season (1 Jul–15 Sep, 09:00–16:00). 9 free spaces for EU disability-permit holders. Off-peak: park freely without booking. Alternative: free bus 442 from Villabassa.

entrance.name: Lago di Braies / Pragser Wildsee shoreline
entrance.transfer: Walk ~5 min south from P4 to the lake shore and Hotel Pragser Wildsee

Lago di Braies (Pragser Wildsee).getting_there: Peak season (1 Jul–15 Sep, 09:00–16:00): road closed — book P4 online in advance or take bus 442 from Villabassa. Off-peak: drive directly to P4 (~€38/day).

parking.name: Passo Tre Croci / Rifugio Tre Croci roadside (1,809 m)
parking.notes: Roadside parking at Passo Tre Croci on SS48 (Cortina–Auronzo di Cadore). Very popular Jul–Aug; lot fills by 08:00–09:00 — arrive early or take the Cortina bus to the pass. No vehicle access beyond the road. Entire route to lake is foot-only.

entrance.name: Lago di Sorapiss (1,923 m) / Rifugio Alfonso Vandelli
entrance.transfer: foot-only — CAI trail 215 (~8 km one-way, ~3.5–4 h ascent, moderate-strenuous)

Lago di Sorapis.getting_there: Drive SS48 (Cortina–Auronzo) to Passo Tre Croci (1,809 m); park near Rifugio Tre Croci. Hike CAI 215 (~3.5–4 h, 8 km one-way) to the lake at 1,923 m. Lot fills by 09:00 in peak season.

parking.name: Parking de Bonlieu (Vieille Ville — underground)
parking.notes: Large underground parking under the Bonlieu cultural centre, ~200 m from old-town canals and Jardins de l'Europe lakefront. Rate estimated. Surface blue-zone metered spots nearby. Very busy July–August.

entrance.name: Jardins de l'Europe / Quai des Vieilles-Prisons (old-town lakefront)
entrance.transfer: 5-min walk north from parking via Rue Jean-Jaurès

Lake Annecy.getting_there: Via A41 → exit Annecy-Centre; follow Vieille Ville / Lac signs. Park at Parking de Bonlieu (~€1.80/h); 5-min walk to old-town canals and Jardins de l'Europe lakefront.

Lake Ashi & Pirate-ship Cruise.notes: Hakone Sightseeing Cruise 'pirate ship'. One-way ¥1,200 adult, round-trip ¥2,220; Special Deck +¥600. Operates 09:30–16:30.
Lake Ashi & Pirate-ship Cruise.getting_there: Hakone Tozan Bus from Hakone-Yumoto to Moto-Hakone-kō (~40 min) or via Hakone Ropeway from Sōunzan to Tōgendai. Hakone Free Pass covers cruise + buses + ropeway.

Lake Lucerne Paddle Steamers.notes: Single-leg adult fares vary by route; covered by Swiss Travel Pass.

Lake Mashū.notes: 1st observation deck free; 3rd deck parking ¥500. Best clear views early morning; lake often shrouded in mist. No access to lakeshore (protected).

parking.name: Lake Mashu 1st viewpoint parking
parking.notes: 1st observation deck lot; ¥500/car in season

Lake Mashū.getting_there: Akan-Mashu NP; ~25 min drive from Kawayu Onsen Station; seasonal bus from Mashu Station in summer.

Landwasser Viaduct.notes: Free viewing platform; train passage is paid (Albula line).

Lavaux Vineyard Terraces.notes: Public terraced paths are free; wine tastings extra.

Le Brévent.notes: Cable-car adult return €43 (2026) ≈ CHF 41; child €36.60; family €133.40.

parking.name: Parking du Brévent (Chamonix)
parking.notes: Small paid lot at 29 Rue Henriette d'Angeville, immediately at the cable car. Rate estimated. Fills by 09:00 in peak summer. Overflow: P+R Centre-Ville / Grépon with free Mulet shuttle bus (line 04, every 30 min). EV charging at Parking Mont-Blanc and Centre-Ville.

entrance.name: Téléphérique du Brévent — base station (29 Rue Henriette d'Angeville)
entrance.transfer: 2-min walk from Parking du Brévent; two-stage cable car: Planpraz 2,000 m → Le Brévent 2,525 m

Le Brévent.getting_there: Via A40 'Autoroute Blanche' → Chamonix; park at Parking du Brévent (~€2.50/h est.) adjacent to cable car, or P+R Centre-Ville for long stays. Adult return €43.

Leukerbad.notes: Walliser Alpentherme three-hour adult thermal ticket; village itself is free to walk.

parking.name: Parkhaus Altstadt Luzern (Baselstrasse 4)
parking.notes: Primary Old Town garage, 24h, ~600 spaces, ~400 m south of the Kapellbrücke. Alternative: Parkhaus Kantonalbank (Pilatusstrasse 2, ~570 spaces, CHF 2.50/hr est.). Old town core is mostly pedestrianised. Train to Luzern HB is a 5-min walk from the bridge.

entrance.name: Kapellbrücke (Chapel Bridge) — Kapellplatz south end
entrance.transfer: ~5-min walk north-east from Altstadt garage to the 14th-century covered wooden bridge

Lucerne Old Town & Kapellbrücke.getting_there: Park in Parkhaus Altstadt, Lucerne (CHF 2/hr est.); 5-min walk to the 14th-century Kapellbrücke and Lucerne's medieval Old Town.

Madonna del Sasso.notes: Sanctuary entry free; funicular from Locarno CHF 8 return.

parking.name: Malga Ciapela cable car base parking (1,450 m)
parking.notes: Large free open-air car park at Malga Ciapela (Rocca Pietore municipality), at the base of the 3-stage Funivia Marmolada gondola. Via SS203 / SP641 from Agordo/Caprile (Belluno/Veneto side). Summer season: 12 Jun – 27 Sep 2026.

entrance.name: Punta Rocca cable-car summit station (3,265 m)
entrance.transfer: 3-stage Funivia Marmolada: Malga Ciapela → Coston d'Antermoja (2,350 m) → Serauta (2,950 m) → Punta Rocca (3,265 m); ~12 min total

Marmolada.getting_there: Drive SS203/SP641 to Malga Ciapela (1,450 m, Rocca Pietore). Free parking at cable car base. 3-stage gondola to Punta Rocca (3,265 m) in ~12 min. Summer Jun–Sep.

Matsumoto Castle (Karasu-jō, 'Crow Castl.notes: Adult ¥700 (combined with Matsumoto City Museum); elem/jr ¥300. Hours: 08:30–17:00 (last entry 16:30); extended 08:00–18:00 during Golden Week & summer. Closed Dec 29–31.

parking.name: Matsumoto Castle Gate Parking
parking.notes: Open 07:30–22:30; ~5 min walk to castle

Matsumoto Castle (Karasu-jō, 'Crow Castl.getting_there: 15 min walk from JR Matsumoto Stn, or Town Sneaker North Loop bus to Matsumotojo-Shiyakushomae (~10 min, ¥200).

Matsushima Bay (Three Great Views).notes: Bay views and shoreline free. Fukuura-jima bridge ¥200; Godaido and Oshima access also small fees. Sightseeing cruises ~¥1,500.
Matsushima Bay (Three Great Views).getting_there: JR Senseki Line to Matsushima-kaigan Station from Sendai (~40 min).

Matterhorn.notes: Mountain itself; viewing from Zermatt is free, lifts to high viewpoints are not.

Meiji Jingū.notes: Shrine grounds free. Hours vary by month: opens sunrise (~05:00–06:40), closes sunset (~16:00–18:30). Inner Garden (Gyoen) ¥500, Treasure Museum ¥1000.
Meiji Jingū.getting_there: 1 min walk from Harajuku Stn (JR Yamanote) or Meiji-jingumae Stn (Tokyo Metro Chiyoda/Fukutoshin).

Mer de Glace & Montenvers Railway.notes: Train-only return €31.50 adult; full package (train + cable car + ice cave + exhibitions) €49.70 (2026).

parking.name: Parking du Biolay (free) / fallback: Parking du Mont Blanc (underground, 239 spaces)
parking.notes: Biolay is the nearest free surface lot on Avenue Cachat le Géant, ~120 m from the Montenvers railway departure. Fills quickly. Fallback: Parking du Mont Blanc underground (~350 m).

entrance.name: Chamonix-Montenvers rack railway valley station (Place de la Mer de Glace)
entrance.transfer: ~120 m walk from Biolay lot; ~350 m from Parking du Mont Blanc

Mer de Glace & Montenvers Railway.getting_there: Park at Biolay (free) or Parking du Mont Blanc on Avenue du Mont Blanc; 2–4-min walk to the Montenvers rack railway station; 20-min cog ride to Montenvers (1,913 m); gondola descends to the Mer de Glace ice cave.

Mont Blanc.notes: Mont Blanc summit (4,806 m) is climber-only. Recommended public viewpoint = Aiguille du Midi (3,842 m). Cable-car return €83 adult ≈ CHF 79.

parking.name: Parking Centre Ville – Grépon (Chamonix)
parking.notes: Same routing as Aiguille du Midi. Mont Blanc itself is foot/technical-climb only — the Aiguille du Midi is the definitive public viewing platform.

entrance.name: Aiguille du Midi cable-car valley station — gateway to Mont Blanc viewpoint
entrance.transfer: ~210 m walk from Grépon parking; cable car 20 min to summit viewpoint

Mont Blanc.getting_there: Mont Blanc (4,806 m) is climber-only; the recommended public viewpoint is the Aiguille du Midi (3,842 m). Park at Grépon, 3-min walk to cable car, ride 20 min to summit. Bring warm layers.

Monte San Giorgio.notes: UNESCO area free; fossil museum in Meride CHF 12.

Morteratsch Glacier.notes: Glacier trail is free; Bernina railway station nearby.

Mount Pilatus.notes: Cogwheel-only return from Alpnachstad; Golden Round Trip from Lucerne ~CHF 109.

parking.name: Parkplatz Alpnachstad — Pilatusbahn valley station
parking.notes: Free surface lot adjacent to the world's steepest cogwheel (440 m). Cogwheel late May–mid-Nov only; year-round alternative is the cable car from Kriens (~CHF 5/day at Krienseregg, bus No. 1 from Lucerne).

entrance.name: Alpnachstad — Pilatusbahn cogwheel valley station (440 m)
entrance.transfer: Adjacent parking; world's steepest cogwheel (48% max grade) 30 min to Pilatus Kulm (2,073 m)

Mount Pilatus.getting_there: Drive to Alpnachstad (free parking adjacent to station); board world's steepest cogwheel (48%, May–Nov) 30 min to Pilatus Kulm (2,073 m). Year-round alternative: cable car from Kriens.

Mount Rigi (Rigi Kulm).notes: Day pass covering all Rigi mountain railways and cable cars.

parking.name: Parkplatz Vitznau — Vitznau-Rigi-Bahn
parking.notes: Municipal lot in Vitznau on Lake Lucerne's south shore, next to Europe's first mountain rack railway (1871). Faster from Zurich/A4: Arth-Goldau P+R + alternate cogwheel — both lines reach the summit.

entrance.name: Vitznau — Vitznau-Rigi-Bahn cogwheel valley station (434 m)
entrance.transfer: ~100 m walk from parking; cogwheel 35 min to Rigi Kulm (1,797 m)

Mount Rigi (Rigi Kulm).getting_there: Park in Vitznau (CHF 10/day est.) at Europe's first mountain railway (1871); 35-min cogwheel to Rigi Kulm (1,797 m) with views over Lakes Lucerne, Zug, and Zürich.

Mount Takao.notes: Hiking trails free. Cable car/chairlift ¥500 one-way, ¥950 round-trip (adult). Operates 08:00–17:15 (seasonal).
Mount Takao.getting_there: Keio Line to Takaosanguchi Station (~50 min from Shinjuku).

parking.name: Titlisbahnen / Gemeindeparkhaus Engelberg
parking.notes: Multi-level covered parking in Engelberg, ~200 m from Titlisbahnen valley station. A2 motorway: exit Stans-Süd. ~30 min from Lucerne / 1 h from Zurich.

entrance.name: Engelberg — Titlisbahnen valley station (1,002 m)
entrance.transfer: ~200 m walk; 4-stage cable car (35 min) to Klein Titlis (3,028 m) including the world's first rotating gondola 'Rotair'

Mount Titlis (Engelberg).getting_there: Park in Engelberg (CHF 15/day est.); 4-stage cable car (35 min) to Klein Titlis (3,028 m), including the world's first rotating gondola 'Rotair'. Open year-round.

Mt Aso Nakadake Crater.notes: Aso Panorama Line toll-road & parking ¥800/car (no per-person fee). Access closed during JMA volcanic alert L2+. Hours: 08:30–17:30 (Mar 20–Oct 31), reduced winter to 09:00–16:30; closed at 18:00.
Mt Aso Nakadake Crater.getting_there: Drive Aso Panorama Line from Aso Stn (~35 min). Public bus: Aso Stn → Asosan Nishi (~40 min).

Mt Fuji (Fuji-san).notes: Yoshida-trail toll ¥4,000/person confirmed on Yamanashi Pref. official site (令和8年/2026 schedule); ¥4,000 per visit, mandatory pre-registration; Yoshida regulated Jul 1–Sep 10 (gate closed 14:00–03:00, 4,000-climber daily cap). Shizuoka adopted similar fees in 2025 for the Fujinomiya/Gotemba/Subashiri trails. Off-season climbing closed.

parking.name: Fuji Subaru Line 5th Station (Yoshida trailhead)
parking.cost: Park-and-ride mandatory in peak: ¥1000 shuttle bus RT
parking.notes: Private cars restricted in climbing season; use Subaru Line P&R lot at Fujihokuroku

Mt Fuji (Fuji-san).getting_there: Yoshida route: Bus from Fujisan Stn or Kawaguchiko Stn to Subaru Line 5th Stn (~60 min).

Mt Hakodate.notes: Ropeway adult RT ¥1,800 (one-way ¥1,200). Hours: 10:00–22:00 (Apr 20–Sep 30, last up 21:30); 10:00–21:00 (Oct 1–Apr 19, last up 20:30). Summit also reachable free by car/hike off-peak.

parking.name: Mt Hakodate Ropeway Sanroku Station Parking

Mt Hakodate.getting_there: Tram to Jujigai stop, ~10 min walk to ropeway base; or city bus from JR Hakodate Stn (~10 min).

Mt Inasa Ropeway Night View.notes: Nagasaki Ropeway adult RT ¥1,250 (one-way ¥730). Hours: 09:00–22:00 daily (last up 21:40). Summit also reachable by car/road free (separate paid Skyway available).

parking.name: Fuchi Shrine Ropeway Parking (free shuttle to ropeway)

Mt Inasa Ropeway Night View.getting_there: Free shuttle bus from major Nagasaki hotels/JR Nagasaki Stn to Fuchi Jinja ropeway base, ~10 min.

Mt Rokkō & Mt Maya Night View.notes: Rokko Cable adult RT ¥1,100 (one-way ¥600). Maya View Line (cable+ropeway) adult RT ¥1,560. Hours: Rokko 07:10–21:00; Maya Ropeway 10:00–17:30 (later on Fri–Sun & summer to 20:50 for night views).
Mt Rokkō & Mt Maya Night View.getting_there: City Bus #16 from Hankyu/JR Rokko Stn to Rokko Cable-shita (~10 min, ¥210), then cable car (~10 min).

Murodō Plateau.notes: Access via Tateyama Kurobe Alpine Route. One-way Tateyama–Ōgizawa ¥9,870; round-trip from Tateyama to Murodō ~¥7,380. Open mid Apr–late Nov; Snow Wall corridor late Apr–Jun.
Murodō Plateau.getting_there: Cablecar + bus from Tateyama Station, or Trolley bus from Ōgizawa side.

Märjelensee.notes: Hiking access free; Eggishorn cable car required to reach trail head.

Mürren.notes: Village free; cable cars from Lauterbrunnen are paid (~CHF 16 return).

Nachi-no-Taki, Kumano Nachi Taisha & Sei.notes: Kumano Nachi Taisha & Seiganto-ji grounds free; closer falls platform ¥300; Three-Storied Pagoda viewing deck ¥300; Taisha Treasure Hall ¥300. Hours: shrines/falls roughly 07:00–16:30 daily.
Nachi-no-Taki, Kumano Nachi Taisha & Sei.getting_there: JR Kii-Katsuura Stn → Kumano Kotsu bus to Nachi-no-taki-mae (~25 min, ¥630).

Nagasaki Peace Park & Atomic Bomb Museum.notes: Peace Park free. Atomic Bomb Museum ¥200 adult. Open 08:30–17:30 (extended summer).
Nagasaki Peace Park & Atomic Bomb Museum.getting_there: Nagasaki tram Line 1/3 to Heiwa-kōen stop.

Nara Park & sika deer.notes: Park free and always open. Surrounding sites (Tōdai-ji ¥800, Kasuga-taisha inner ¥700, Kōfuku-ji halls ¥700) charge separately. Deer crackers ¥200.
Nara Park & sika deer.getting_there: Kintetsu Nara Station (~5 min walk) or JR Nara Station (~20 min walk).

Nijō Castle & Ninomaru Palace.notes: Castle + Ninomaru Palace combo ¥1,300 adult. Open 08:45–16:00 (last entry); palace 08:45–15:40. UNESCO World Heritage.
Nijō Castle & Ninomaru Palace.getting_there: Tozai subway to Nijōjō-mae Station; city bus 9/50/101.

Nikkō Tōshōgū.notes: Adult ¥1,600 (jr/elem ¥550) for shrine main precinct. Treasure Museum +¥1,000 (combo ¥2,400). Hours: 09:00–17:00 (Apr 1–Oct 31); 09:00–16:00 (Nov 1–Mar 31); last entry 30 min prior.
Nikkō Tōshōgū.getting_there: From JR/Tobu Nikko Stn, World Heritage Loop Bus to Omotesando (~10 min); or 35 min walk uphill.

Ninna-ji (Omuro Cherries & Five-Storey P.notes: Goten Palace ¥800; cherry season (Apr) Omuro garden ¥500 extra. Open 09:00–17:00 (Mar–Nov), 09:00–16:30 (Dec–Feb).
Ninna-ji (Omuro Cherries & Five-Storey P.getting_there: Randen Kitano Line to Omuro-Ninnaji Station; bus 26 from Kyoto Station.

Niseko United.notes: All-Mountain day pass ~¥9,500 (winter); summer gondola/lifts ~¥2,000. Four interconnected resorts.
Niseko United.getting_there: JR Hakodate Line to Kutchan/Niseko Station; bus shuttle from New Chitose Airport (~3 hr).

Noboribetsu Jigokudani.notes: Hell Valley walkways free, always open. Onsen day-use varies (¥1,000–2,500). Illumination evenings in summer.
Noboribetsu Jigokudani.getting_there: JR Muroran Line to Noboribetsu Station + bus (~15 min) to Onsen.

Nyūtō Onsen-kyō (Tsurunoyu).notes: Day-use bathing ¥800 adult; outdoor mixed bath 10:00–15:00 only (no day-use Mon). Famous milky-white sulphur waters.
Nyūtō Onsen-kyō (Tsurunoyu).getting_there: Bus from Tazawako Station (~50 min) to Nyūtō Onsen; rough mountain road.

Oeschinensee (Oeschinen Lake).notes: Kandersteg–Oeschinen gondola return; lake itself is free if hiked up.

parking.name: Oeschinensee Cable Car valley station parking (Kandersteg)
parking.notes: Open from 06:00. Max vehicle 5 m × 2 m. 8 × 11 kW EV chargers in P2. Fills by midday on summer weekends — arrive early. Campers use Schützenhaus / Sunnbüel.

entrance.name: Kandersteg – Oeschinensee Cable Car valley station
entrance.transfer: Gondola (~5 min) + 25–30-min walk to lake shore at 1,578 m; electric taxi available in summer

Oeschinensee (Oeschinen Lake).getting_there: Park at the Oeschinensee gondola station in Kandersteg; ride the gondola (~5 min); walk 25–30 min to the UNESCO-listed lake shore at 1,578 m.

Oirase Stream.notes: 14km riverside trail free, always open. Peak foliage mid-late October. Eco-shuttle bus from Yakeyama in autumn.

parking.name: Free roadside lots
parking.notes: Free roadside lots at Ishigedo, Nenokuchi

Oirase Stream.getting_there: JR bus from Aomori/Hachinohe Station to Yakeyama or Nenokuchi (seasonal Apr–Nov).

Okama Crater Lake (Mt Zaō).notes: Zaō Echo Line free; Zaō Highline toll ¥380/car to Okama viewing area. Road closed Nov–Apr. Lake viewable 5 min walk from lot.
Okama Crater Lake (Mt Zaō).getting_there: Drive via Zaō Echo Line + Highline; or summer bus from Yamagata Station.

Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium & Ocean Expo P.notes: Aquarium adult ¥2,180 (high-school ¥1,440, elem/jr ¥710); discounted ¥1,510 after 16:00. Ocean Expo Park grounds free. Hours: 08:30–18:30 (last entry 17:30); extended to 20:00 in summer peak.

parking.name: Ocean Expo Park P7 (closest to aquarium)

Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium & Ocean Expo P.getting_there: Yanbaru Express Bus from Naha Airport to Kinen-Koen-mae stop (~2h 30min, ¥2,100); or rental car ~2hr from Naha.

Okinawa Peace Memorial Park & Himeyuri M.notes: Park free. Prefectural Peace Museum ¥300; Himeyuri Peace Museum ¥450 (nearby). Open 09:00–17:00.
Okinawa Peace Memorial Park & Himeyuri M.getting_there: Bus 89 from Naha BT to Itoman + local bus 82; or rental car ~40 min from Naha.

parking.name: Parcheggio Piazza Motta (lakeside, below ZTL historic centre)
parking.notes: Main lakeside car park at Piazza Motta, outside the ZTL pedestrian historic core. Free lots above town on Via Panoramica. Short walk to the waterfront. Public rowboats ('barcaioli') to Isola San Giulio (~300 m, ~2 min, ~€3 pp return).

entrance.name: Piazza Motta waterfront / village boat pier
entrance.transfer: foot from parking; rowboat (~2 min, ~€3 pp return) to Isola San Giulio

Orta San Giulio.getting_there: Drive to Orta San Giulio; park in Piazza Motta lakeside lot (~€2/hr) or free lots above town. Walk to waterfront. Boatmen row visitors to Isola San Giulio (~300 m, ~€3 pp).

Osaka Castle (Ōsaka-jō).notes: Castle keep (museum) ¥600 adult; grounds free. Open 09:00–17:00 (last entry 16:30); extended in spring/golden week.
Osaka Castle (Ōsaka-jō).getting_there: JR Loop Line to Osakajōkōen; or Tanimachi/Chuo subway.

Osorezan Bodai-ji.notes: Entry ¥700 adult. Open 06:00–18:00, May 1–Oct 31 only (closed in winter). Sulphurous volcanic 'gateway to afterlife'.
Osorezan Bodai-ji.getting_there: Bus from Mutsu BT (~45 min, seasonal); ~30 min drive from Mutsu.

Otaru Canal & Sakaimachi.notes: Public canal walk and Sakaimachi historic street free, accessible 24h. Individual museums/shops have own fees. Winter Snow Light Path Festival (Feb) free.
Otaru Canal & Sakaimachi.getting_there: 10 min walk from JR Otaru Stn down Chuo-dori to the canal; JR from Sapporo ~40 min (¥750).

parking.name: San Martino di Castrozza — Colverde gondola base (1,444 m)
parking.notes: Paid and free parking throughout San Martino di Castrozza. Colverde gondola base at south end of village. Two-stage ascent: Colverde gondola → Col Verde (2,162 m), then Rosetta cable car → Rifugio Rosetta (2,743 m). Estimated adult return ~€20–25 (low confidence).

entrance.name: Rifugio Rosetta (2,743 m) / Col Verde plateau
entrance.transfer: Colverde gondola → Col Verde (2,162 m) + Rosetta cable car → 2,743 m; ~20 min combined

Pale di San Martino.getting_there: Drive SP347/SS50 to San Martino di Castrozza (1,444 m); park in village. Colverde gondola to Col Verde (2,162 m); Rosetta cable car to Rifugio Rosetta (2,743 m). Gateway to the massif.

Rapperswil Castle & Old Town.notes: Castle grounds and old town free; Polish Museum CHF 8.

Reichenbach Falls.notes: Reichenbachfall-Bahn funicular return; falls themselves are free to view from the valley.

Rhine Falls (Rheinfall).notes: Schloss Laufen viewing platform; boat trips ~CHF 8–25 extra.

Riffelsee.notes: Lake free; reached via Gornergrat cogwheel (paid).

Ritsurin Garden.notes: Adult ¥410. Open ~sunrise–sunset (seasonal, ~07:00–17:00). One of Japan's finest gardens, designated Special Place of Scenic Beauty.
Ritsurin Garden.getting_there: JR Kotoku Line to Ritsurin-kōen-Kitaguchi Station (~3 min walk).

Ruinaulta (Rhine Gorge).notes: Hiking and viewing free; train via Rhaetian Railway is paid.

Ryōan-ji Rock Garden.notes: Hours: 08:00–17:00 (Mar–Nov), 08:30–16:30 (Dec–Feb). Adult ¥600, students ¥500, children ¥300 per official temple site.

parking.name: Ryōan-ji Temple Parking
parking.notes: Free for visitors using the temple; ~80 spaces near main gate.

Ryōan-ji Rock Garden.getting_there: From Kyoto Stn: City Bus 50 to Ritsumeikan-daigaku-mae or JR Bus to Ryōan-ji-mae (~30 min). Or Keifuku Randen Kitano line to Ryōanji Stn, 7-min walk.

Rütli Meadow.notes: Meadow free; reached by paid lake boat.

Saas-Fee.notes: Village is car-free and free to enter; lifts and Allalin extra.

parking.name: Parking near funicular lower station — Via Scarognini, Varallo
parking.notes: Street parking and small lots near the funicular lower station in Varallo Sesia. Limited roadside parking also accessible by road directly to the Sacro Monte (Via Sacro Monte). Funicular return ~€3–4 (unverified). Sacro Monte sits 600 m a.s.l., 150 m above the town. UNESCO World Heritage (2003).

entrance.name: Sacro Monte di Varallo (600 m) — basilica + 45 chapels
entrance.transfer: funicular from Varallo town (~5 min); or scenic pilgrim path on foot (~20 min)

Sacro Monte di Varallo.getting_there: Drive to Varallo Sesia (Valsesia); park near funicular lower station in town. Funicular (~5 min, ~€3–4 return) or foot path to Sacro Monte (600 m). UNESCO heritage site.

Sagano Scenic Railway (Romantic Train).notes: One-way ¥880 adult, 25 min Torokko Saga ↔ Kameoka. Operates ~09:00–16:00, closed Wed (and Dec 30–Feb). Pre-book in cherry/koyo seasons.
Sagano Scenic Railway (Romantic Train).getting_there: JR Saga-Arashiyama Station, adjacent to Torokko Saga Station.

Sakurajima Volcano.notes: Hours: Ferry runs 24h (reduced overnight from 2025-10-01). Sakurajima Ferry adult one-way ¥250, child ¥130 (Kagoshima City Ship Dept). Mountain itself free; trails/observatories open.
Sakurajima Volcano.getting_there: From Kagoshima-Chuo Stn: city bus or tram to Kagoshima Port (~15 min), then Sakurajima Ferry (15 min crossing, every 15-20 min daytime).

parking.name: Parkplatz P2 – near Salzbergseilbahn Talstation (Hallstatt-Lahn)
parking.notes: ⚠️ CLOSED since 1 September 2025 for funicular reconstruction; reopening expected summer 2026. Alternative: daily shuttle bus from Hallstatt to Salzwelten Altaussee mine. P2 is seasonal (spring–autumn).

entrance.name: Salzwelten Hallstatt – Bergstation Salt Mine Entrance (~850 m)
entrance.transfer: Standseilbahn (funicular) from Hallstatt-Lahn — CURRENTLY UNDER RECONSTRUCTION

Salzwelten Hallstatt – World's Oldest Sa.getting_there: ⚠️ Closed until summer 2026 for funicular reconstruction. When open: park at P2, 1-min walk to the Salzbergseilbahn valley station, ride the funicular to the mine entrance.

Sanjūsangen-dō.notes: Adult ¥600. Open 08:30–17:00 (Apr–Nov 15), 09:00–16:00 (Nov 16–Mar). 1,001 Kannon statues; National Treasure hall.

parking.name: Free temple lot
parking.notes: Limited

Sanjūsangen-dō.getting_there: Keihan Shichijō Station (~7 min walk); city bus 100/206/208.

parking.name: St. Wolfgang Municipal Car Park (near Schafbergbahn ErlebnisQuartier)
parking.notes: Municipal lots in St. Wolfgang am Wolfgangsee, walking distance to the ErlebnisQuartier valley station. Railway runs daily 25 Apr – 1 Nov 2026.

entrance.name: Schafbergbahn ErlebnisQuartier Valley Station (St. Wolfgang, 542 m)
entrance.transfer: 35-min steam cogwheel rack railway to Schafbergspitze (1,732 m); optional Schafbergalm mid-station (1,363 m, 30 min)

Schafbergbahn – Steam Cogwheel Railway.getting_there: Drive to St. Wolfgang am Wolfgangsee; park near the ErlebnisQuartier valley station. Board the historic steam rack railway for a 35-min climb to the summit. Runs Apr 25 – Nov 1.

parking.name: Stechelberg – Schilthornbahn valley station parking
parking.notes: Free outdoor parking at the end of the Lauterbrunnen Valley road. Overflows on busy summer days — alternative paid parking in Lauterbrunnen village + postbus.

entrance.name: Stechelberg – Schilthornbahn valley station
entrance.transfer: Stechelberg–Mürren cable car (world's steepest 159.4%, Dec 2024) to car-free Mürren, then cable car via Birg to Schilthorn (~45 min total)

Schilthorn / Piz Gloria.getting_there: Drive to Stechelberg (free parking, end of the valley road); board the world's steepest cable car to car-free Mürren; change cable car via Birg for the final ascent to Piz Gloria at 2,970 m.

Seceda.notes: Adult return Ortisei → Seceda €74 (2026, confirmed seceda.it) ≈ CHF 70.

parking.name: Ortisei village parking near Talstation Seceda
parking.notes: Multiple public car parks in Ortisei (St. Ulrich) near the gondola valley station. Gondola: 2-stage Ortisei → Furnes → Seceda ridge. Dogs €7, bikes €7.

entrance.name: Seceda upper gondola station / ridge viewpoint (2,518 m)
entrance.transfer: 2-stage gondola: Ortisei → Furnes (~12 min) + Furnes → Seceda (~13 min)

Seceda.getting_there: Drive to Ortisei (Val Gardena); park near the gondola valley station; 2-stage cable car to Seceda ridge. Adult return €74 (2026).

parking.name: Parkplatz Seis-Seiser Alm Bahn (Schlernstraße 39, Seis am Schlern)
parking.notes: Large outdoor lot is FREE; underground garages charge a fee. 3 EV charging stations. Road to the plateau is CLOSED 09:00–17:00 to non-residents while the cable car runs (May–Nov).

entrance.name: Compatsch (Seiser Alm gondola top station, ~1,850 m)
entrance.transfer: Seiser Alm Aerial Cableway ~15 min from Seis am Schlern (peak 08:00–19:00, summer 22 May – 2 Nov)

Seiser Alm (Alpe di Siusi).getting_there: Park free at Seis am Schlern gondola station (Schlernstraße 39, outdoor). Road to Seiser Alm plateau is closed 09:00–17:00 for non-residents — use the gondola (~15 min).

Senjōjiki Cirque & Komagatake Ropeway.notes: Bus + ropeway round-trip from Komagane ¥4,200 adult (bus ¥1,650 + ropeway ¥2,540 combo). Ropeway 08:00–17:00; weather-dependent.
Senjōjiki Cirque & Komagatake Ropeway.getting_there: JR Iida Line to Komagane Station + dedicated bus to Shirabidaira + ropeway to Senjōjiki.

Sensō-ji & Kaminarimon (Asakusa).notes: Hours: Main Hall 06:00–17:00 (06:30 Oct–Mar); grounds and Kaminarimon open 24h. Free admission. Nakamise shops daytime only.
Sensō-ji & Kaminarimon (Asakusa).getting_there: Asakusa Stn (Ginza, Asakusa, Tōbu, Tsukuba Express lines), 1–5-min walk to Kaminarimon. From Tokyo Stn ~20 min via Ginza line.

Shibuya Crossing & Hachikō Statue.notes: Hours: 24h public street; Hachikō statue accessible anytime. Free. Best viewed evenings; Shibuya Scramble Square SHIBUYA SKY (paid, separate) offers overhead view.
Shibuya Crossing & Hachikō Statue.getting_there: Shibuya Stn (JR Yamanote, Ginza, Hanzōmon, Fukutoshin, Tōkyū, Keiō lines) — Hachikō exit opens directly onto the crossing.

Shibuya Sky.notes: Online timed-entry ¥3,000; walk-up ¥3,200. Hours 10:00–22:30 (last entry 21:20). Open-air rooftop closes in bad weather.
Shibuya Sky.getting_there: JR/Metro Shibuya Station, directly connected via Shibuya Scramble Square B2/B3.

Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden.notes: Adult ¥500. Hours 09:00–18:00 (seasonal extensions during sakura). Closed Mondays (open daily late-Mar–Apr & Nov).

parking.name: Okido Gate parking
parking.notes: Then ¥200/30min; ~200 spots.

Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden.getting_there: Shinjukugyoenmae Station (Marunouchi Line) 5 min walk; or JR Sendagaya.
